summary: Session 2 of 3 -- Net-new content-aware close-out wiring. Shipped the tier-orthogonal Path-Aware Critique close-out GATE that enforces the Session-1 policy attribute. (1) GATE VALIDATOR: ai_router/path_aware_critique.py gains validate_path_aware_critique_gate() + PathAwareCritiqueGateResult -- a posture-agnostic, never-raising, tier-orthogonal validator that reads the durable pathAwareCritique record (none -> no-op; advisory/required -> locate path-aware-critique.json beside spec.md and run the S1 multi-provider validator). It deliberately MIRRORS validate_dedicated_verification's shape (Set 057 Q6) rather than reusing it -- the Erratum proved that gate is Lightweight-only (gates on verificationMode), so this wiring is NET-NEW on the Full-tier close path. (2) WIRING: close_session.run() gained the gate after the dedicated_verification gate, before the success path: fires ONLY on the set-terminal close (_close_is_terminal), required -> HARD-block in an interactive TTY / SOFT-warn non-TTY/headless/--accept-suggestions (the Set 057 Q6 fail-posture), advisory -> ALWAYS soft-warn never block, none -> skip; ASCII-only; fail-open in the non-block direction (the module import lives INSIDE the broad try/except so any import-time failure skips the gate rather than wedging close-out). emits closeout_failed with failed_checks=["path_aware_critique_gate"] on a hard block. (3) TESTS: 19 new (13 close-gate end-to-end across level x tier {full,lightweight} + TTY/headless/--accept-suggestions fail-posture + single-provider/trivial/missing-artifact blocks on required + advisory-never-blocks + non-terminal-close-skips + unrecorded-default-skips; 6 gate-validator units). Full ai_router suite green: 1391 passed / 1 skipped (S1 was 1372). (4) DOCS: ai_router/docs/close-out.md (new step 7b documenting the three content-aware close-out gates) + docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md (a path-aware-critique close-out-gate paragraph paralleling the Set 057 Q6 note). Cross-provider session verification (gpt-5.4): Round 1 ISSUES_FOUND -- 1 Major (the module import sat OUTSIDE the broad fail-open try/except, so a non-ImportError import failure could wedge close-out, contradicting the fail-open contract) which was FIXED (import moved inside the guard, a deliberate strengthening over the mirrored Set 057 block), and 1 Minor (apparent doc mojibake) DISPROVEN as a cp1252 harness artifact -- the files on disk are valid UTF-8 (cat -v shows 0xC2A7 section-sign and 0xE28094 em dash, consistent with the surrounding docs), the mojibake existed only in the verifier's cp1252-decoded diff; Round 2 (UTF-8 diff) VERIFIED. No release this session (the PyPI ai_router release carrying this gate is Session 3). The manual-workflow docs, reusable prompt template, and the dogfood critique artifact for this set are Session 3.

reason.specifics: Routed recommendation (cross-provider analysis, to reduce self-bias). The analysis recommended a fresh-eyes engine over continuity for Session 3: 'the primary deliverables are canonical documentation, a reusable prompt template, and a dogfooded critique artifact, culminating in a public release. Using a different top-tier model to interpret the existing implementation and author the canonical workflow serves as a powerful real-world test of the system's clarity and usability -- if an entirely different orchestrator can comprehend the architecture, produce high-quality docs, and generate a valid critique artifact that passes the S1/S2 gates, that is strong evidence the system is robust and not reliant on implicit model-specific context. This cross-provider validation is critical before a public release and outweighs continuity.' Model normalized from the routed 'gpt-4o' (not in this repo's orchestrator roster) to the rostered top-tier OpenAI engine gpt-5-4; the operator confirms the actual S3 orchestrator, noting that the PyPI publish runbook (git tag push / gh CLI / green-Test prerequisite) is operator-driven regardless of engine.
